prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene (C₆H₅NO₂) using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in the gas phase at charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet). Geometry: input user-supplied .xyz file; single-point Fukui calculation (no geometry re-optimization). Mulliken partial charges computed for the neutral, cation (N−1, M=2), and anion (N+1, M=2) states to derive Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic character), f⁻ (nucleophilic character), and dual descriptor Δf = f⁺ − f⁻. The calculation was performed in PySCF and converged for all three electronic states. Fukui charge conservation: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000 and Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000, both passing internal verification checks. The most electrophilic site is the ipso-carbon (atom 4, ortho to the nitro group) with f⁺ = 0.49642 and dual descriptor Δf = 0.51070, indicating activation toward nucleophilic aromatic substitution. The most nucleophilic site is the meta-carbon (atom 5) with f⁻ = 0.10729. All Fukui indices and Mulliken charges per atom are reported in the results section above. Integrity status: trustworthy, all convergence and conservation checks passed.
